sweeps.summary: Six items added: PFE, PLTR and PYPL Q1 2026 earnings; the May 4 Iranian drone strike on UAE's Fujairah oil hub that pushed Brent +5.8%; the 30Y UST yield breaching 5% for the first time since July 2025; and the third straight day of U.S. spot bitcoin ETF net inflows ($532M).

added.title: Pfizer Q1 2026 — beats on EPS and revenue, reaffirms full-year outlook
added.note: Mega-cap pharma earnings beat with reaffirmed FY guidance — primary-source confirmed.

added.title: Palantir Q1 2026 — revenue +85% Y/Y, raises FY26 guide to 71% growth, stock dips on valuation
added.note: 85% Y/Y growth + 10pt guide raise, but next-day stock dropped ~3.8% — earnings + mover.

added.title: PayPal Q1 2026 — beats top and bottom but stock plunges ~10% on cautious guidance
added.note: Beat Q1 but ~10% premarket drop on cautious 2026 transaction-margin guidance — earnings + mover.

added.title: Iran drone strike sets fire at UAE Fujairah oil hub; Brent jumps 5.8% to $114
added.note: First Iranian strike on UAE since April 8 ceasefire; Brent +5.8% to $114.44 — primary geopolitical+commodity.

added.title: 30-year Treasury yield breaches 5% for first time since July 2025 as oil-driven inflation bites
added.note: 30Y yield closed above 5% (5.021%) for first time since July 2025 — major rates milestone.

added.title: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs draw $532M on May 4 — third straight day of net inflows
added.note: Third straight day of net inflows totaling $532M led by BlackRock IBIT — notable crypto flow signal.

sweeps.summary: Four new releases: Pinterest's Q1 beat + raised guide + ~$2B buyback (~+15%), Shopify's Q1 revenue beat with a slower-growth Q2 guide (~-7%), Duolingo's Q1 beat undercut by a 10.5% FY bookings guide (~-13%), and Hubbell's $3B cash acquisition of NSI Industries.

added.title: Pinterest Q1 2026 — revenue +18%, raised Q2 guide and $2B buyback send shares ~15% higher
added.note: Beat + raise + $2B buyback with ~+15% move clears the major bar (≥3% single-name and ≥2 tier-1 outlets).

added.title: Shopify Q1 2026 — revenue +34% with $100B GMV but stock falls ~7% as Q2 guide signals slower growth
added.note: Mega-cap-relevant SaaS with named guidance catalyst and ~7% sell-off; primary IR press release plus 8-K available.

added.title: Duolingo Q1 2026 — revenue +27% but full-year bookings guide of 10.5% growth knocks shares ~13%
added.note: Named bookings-guide catalyst with ~13% move clears mover bar; primary press release on globenewswire plus SEC 8-K.

added.title: Hubbell to acquire NSI Industries from Sentinel for $3B in cash, betting on electrification and datacenter buildout
added.note: $3B all-cash deal with ~15.5x EBITDA multiple disclosed via Hubbell's official press release and 8-K — clean primary M&A item.

sweeps.summary: Three additions: GameStop's unsolicited $56B bid for eBay (May 3); Amazon's launch of Amazon Supply Chain Services (May 4) that knocked UPS ~10% and FedEx ~9%; and Tyson Foods' FQ2 2026 beat with a raised FY26 guide.

added.title: GameStop offers ~$56B unsolicited bid for eBay at $125/share, half cash and half stock
added.note: Unsolicited $56B bid disclosed publicly by GameStop on May 3, with eBay board acknowledging receipt; widely covered tier-1 today.

added.title: Amazon opens its full logistics network to outside businesses, sending UPS ~10% lower and FedEx ~9% lower
added.note: Sector-defining launch with named anchor customers and clear single-day moves in UPS and FDX.

added.title: Tyson Foods FQ2 2026 — adj EPS $0.87 beats $0.76, raises FY26 op income guide; shares jump ~8%
added.note: Beat consensus, raised FY26 guidance, ~+8% on the day.

sweeps.summary: Four adds: AMD Q1 2026 (beat + raised Q2 guide on 57% Data Center growth), a Bloomberg-sourced rumor that Apple is exploring Intel and Samsung as US chipmaking alternatives (INTC +13%), Micron crossing $700B in market cap on the AI-memory rally, and Ferrari Q1 2026 (EPS beat, full-year guide reaffirmed).

added.title: AMD Q1 2026 — revenue $10.25B, data center +57%; Q2 guide $11.2B above Street
added.note: Mega-cap AI semis print just after the close on May 5; revenue and Q2 guide both above consensus, Data Center revenue +57% — primary AI-accelerator read for the quarter.

added.title: Apple reportedly in early talks with Intel and Samsung on US chipmaking; INTC closes ~+13%
added.note: Bloomberg-sourced rumor of Apple exploring Intel/Samsung as US foundry alternatives; INTC closed +12.9% on the report — clear single-name catalyst-driven mover.

added.title: Micron tops $700B market cap as memory rally accelerates; MU surges ~11%
added.note: First time Micron has crossed $700B in market cap; CNBC ran the milestone as a story today and the broader memory complex rallied alongside.

added.title: Ferrari Q1 2026 — adj EPS €2.33 beats, full-year 2026 guide reaffirmed
added.note: Q1 2026 print on May 5 with EPS and revenue ahead of consensus and full-year guide reaffirmed — distinct sector (European luxury auto) versus today's other prints.

sweeps.summary: Two adds: Astera Labs Q1 2026 earnings beat with a strong Q2 guide (AI-infra read-through), and the launch of the U.S. Navy's 'Project Freedom' Hormuz escort, which let Brent give back most of Monday's geopolitical spike.

added.title: Astera Labs Q1 2026 — revenue $308.4M (+93% Y/Y), strong Q2 guide, ALAB +8.8% on the day
added.note: Clean major-tier AI-infra earnings beat with a guide raise, +93% Y/Y revenue and +8.8% intraday move; not in feed.

added.title: U.S. Navy 'Project Freedom' Hormuz escort begins; Brent retraces ~4% to ~$110 as Iran ceasefire holds
added.note: Distinct event from the Fujairah strike already in feed — this is the U.S. military response and the resulting ~4% retracement in Brent as the ceasefire holds.

sweeps.summary: Two fresh single-name earnings stories from Tuesday's after-hours: Lucid badly missed the Street on Q1 revenue with widening losses, and AMC posted its strongest Q1 EBITDA since 2019 on a 21% revenue beat.

added.title: Lucid Q1 2026 — revenue $282M misses big on $440M est, adj loss $2.82/share, GAAP gross margin -110%
added.note: Earnings miss with -6.3% intraday move, large Street-revenue gap, Uber 35K robotaxi context — clear single-name catalyst.

added.title: AMC Q1 2026 — revenue +21% to $1.05B, best Q1 EBITDA since 2019; AMC closes +9.7% on box-office strength
added.note: Revenue beat + best Q1 EBITDA since 2019; +9.7% close ahead of print confirms broad market interest in the box-office recovery thesis.

sweeps.summary: Three new earnings-day items: Devon Energy's Q1 miss arrives the same week as the Coterra merger close (~May 7); Fiserv beats EPS but tanks ~9% on a revenue miss and 4% organic decline; Primoris cuts FY guidance on renewables-project pain and gaps ~31% lower AH.

added.title: Devon Q1 2026 misses on revenue and EPS; Coterra all-stock merger expected to close on or around May 7
added.note: Q1 miss + imminent Coterra all-stock close = clean energy-sector M&A + earnings combo within 72h.

added.title: Fiserv Q1 2026 — adj EPS $1.79 beats but revenue and organic growth miss; FISV closes ~9% lower
added.note: Mega-cap fintech, ~9% one-day move with named earnings catalyst, multiple tier-1 takes.

added.title: Primoris Q1 2026 — revenue and EPS miss, FY26 guidance cut on weak renewables projects; PRIM falls ~31% in after-hours
added.note: Mid-cap industrials with ~31% AH move + FY26 guide cut on renewables project execution; narrow but high-conviction notable.

sweeps.summary: Added 4 items from the May 5 PM / May 6 AM cycle: Samsung's $1T market-cap milestone (KOSPI +5.4% on AI memory), Sterling Infrastructure's +52% Q1 beat-and-raise on data-center/semis-fab backlog, Marathon Petroleum's refining-margin blowout with a $5B buyback bump, and Supermicro's mixed FQ3 with a margin-recovery story.

added.title: Samsung Electronics tops $1T market cap as AI memory rally lifts shares ~12%; KOSPI jumps 5.4% to record
added.note: Historic milestone: Samsung becomes second Asian company to cross $1T, with KOSPI rallying 5.4% on AI memory demand — broad-market relevance, not yet in feed.

added.title: Sterling Infrastructure Q1 2026 — adj EPS $3.59 trounces $2.28 est, raises FY26 guide ~30%; STRL closes +52%
added.note: Q1 beat-and-raise drove a 52% intraday move and a fresh 52-week high; primary read-through to AI-infrastructure capex flowing to specialty builders.

added.title: Marathon Petroleum Q1 2026 — adj EPS $1.65 crushes $0.75 est on refining margins, adds $5B to buyback; MPC at 52-week high
added.note: Largest US refiner posts +120% EPS beat and adds $5B to buyback authorization on Iran-driven refining margins; new 52-week high; complements existing oil/Iran narrative.

added.title: Supermicro FQ3 2026 — adj EPS $0.84 beats but revenue $10.2B misses $12.4B est; AI GPU >80% of revenue
added.note: AI-server bellwether: $10.2B revenue +123% Y/Y but a $2B miss; non-GAAP gross margin recovers to 10.1%, important read-through for hyperscaler AI spend cadence.

sweeps.summary: Searched today's earnings (Disney, Uber, Novo Nordisk, Marriott reported pre-market; AppLovin/Arm/DoorDash AC) plus macro/M&A/crypto. Only Novo Nordisk Q1 was indexed and primary-source verifiable at sweep timestamp. Disney/Uber too fresh to verify numbers; deferred to next sweep.

added.title: Novo Nordisk Q1 2026 — Wegovy pill drives 'strongest-ever' GLP-1 launch, raises FY26 guide; NVO +6.7% in Copenhagen
added.note: Largest verified market-mover at sweep time: NVO +6.7% Copenhagen on Wegovy-pill blowout and FY26 guide raise; reframes the Lilly Foundayo competitive narrative.

sweeps.summary: Three fresh, primary-source-confirmed items for May 6, 2026: NVIDIA + Corning announce multi-year AI optical fiber partnership with $500M warrant deal (GLW +14% premarket); Disney FQ2 beats with streaming op income +88%; Uber Q1 prints +25% bookings growth with raised Q2 guide.

added.title: NVIDIA + Corning sign multi-year AI optics partnership; NVDA buys $500M in GLW warrants, GLW surges ~14% premarket
added.note: Major AI infrastructure deal — $500M warrant, 10x US manufacturing buildout, 3 new plants, GLW +14% premarket. Confirmed by NVDA, GLW press releases and 8-K.

added.title: Disney FQ2 2026 — revenue $25.2B beats, streaming op income +88% to $582M; raises FY26 EPS guide to ~12%
added.note: Mega-cap earnings beat with raised FY26 guide and streaming op income +88% Y/Y. DIS +5% premarket.

added.title: Uber Q1 2026 — gross bookings $53.7B (+25%) beat, adj EBITDA +33% to $2.5B; Q2 guide above Street; UBER +10%
added.note: Mega-cap earnings beat: 25% bookings growth, 33% EBITDA growth, Q2 guide above Street; UBER +10%.

sweeps.summary: Three adds: Brent crashes below $100 as Trump pauses Project Freedom and US/Iran near a 14-point peace memo; Marriott Q1 beats and raises FY26 guide; Strategy posts a $12.54B Q1 loss with Saylor floating selective BTC sales for the first time.

added.title: Brent crashes below $100 (-8.2%), WTI -9.8% as Trump pauses Project Freedom; US-Iran near 14-point peace MOU
added.note: Distinct event from the May 5 'Project Freedom escort begins' item: today's news is the OPPOSITE — Trump paused the escort and Brent crashed 8% on peace-memo progress.

added.title: Marriott Q1 2026 — adj EPS $2.72 vs $2.32 prior, RevPAR +4.2%, EBITDA +15% to $1.4B; raises FY26 guide, MAR ~+2%
added.note: Mega-cap lodging beat with raised guide; not yet covered.

added.title: Strategy Q1 2026 — $12.54B net loss on BTC mark-down, holdings reach 818,334 BTC; Saylor opens door to selective bitcoin sales
added.note: First time Strategy publicly signaled BTC may be sold to fund dividends; biggest US corporate bitcoin holder.

sweeps.summary: Two fresh additions: Arista Networks Q1 2026 beat-and-raise that still drew a 10% selloff on multi-year gross-margin warnings, and Apollo's $1T AUM milestone with record fee earnings and a daily-pricing pledge for private credit.

added.title: Arista Q1 2026 — revenue $2.71B beats, raises FY26 to $11.5B and AI to $3.5B; ANET still drops ~10% on margin pressure
added.note: ANET fell ~10% after-hours despite a beat-and-raise — gross-margin warnings flagged by management triggered profit-taking on a name +87% YTD-prior; clear named-catalyst mover with primary IR source.

added.title: Apollo Q1 2026 — AUM tops $1T at $1.03T on record $115B inflows, FRE record $728M; pledges daily pricing for credit funds
added.note: Apollo crossed $1T AUM with record FRE and announced daily pricing for $830B+ of private credit — Bloomberg-led wall of coverage, primary IR source confirmed.

sweeps.summary: Three earnings adds for the May 6 sweep: DoorDash Q1 (margin beat, ~+12% AH), Sphere Entertainment Q1 (Wizard of Oz drives 70% segment growth, ~+8% to record), and Snap Q1 (cautious guidance + ended Perplexity deal, ~-4% AH).

added.title: DoorDash Q1 2026 — adj EPS $0.42 beats $0.36, GOV $31.6B (+37%) but revenue misses; DASH ~+12% AH
added.note: AMC May 6 print: EPS beat, revenue miss, ~+12% AH on margin strength + Q2 guidance.

added.title: Sphere Entertainment Q1 2026 — revenue $386M (+38%), Sphere segment +70% on Wizard of Oz; SPHR ~+8% to record
added.note: AMC May 5 print: revenue +38%, Sphere segment +70%, stock to record on Wizard of Oz traction.

added.title: Snap Q1 2026 — revenue $1.53B (+12%), DAUs +5%; ends $400M Perplexity deal, SNAP ~-4% AH
added.note: AMC May 6 print: revenue +12% but ended $400M Perplexity deal and pulled cautious Q2; ~-4% AH.

sweeps.summary: One add: Arm Q4 FY26 — record print and an above-consensus Q1 FY27 guide, but the AH tape gave back a 12% spike on AGI CPU supply/cost concerns. Most other May 6 movers were already in the feed from the prior sweep.

added.title: Arm Q4 FY26 — revenue $1.49B beats, Q1 FY27 guide $1.26B above Street; ARM +13.6% during day, then reverses ~-5% AH on AGI CPU cost concerns
added.note: Material AI-semis print: closed +13.6% on the day; AH initially +12% then reversed -5.5% on AGI CPU manufacturing cost / supply commentary.

sweeps.summary: Two Q1 2026 single-name prints land within the 72h window: AppLovin posts a 59% Y/Y revenue beat with raised Q2 guide, and Whirlpool's revenue miss + ongoing EPS swing-to-loss sends shares down ~19.5%.

added.title: AppLovin Q1 2026 — revenue $1.84B (+59%) beats, GAAP EPS $3.56, Q2 guide $1.93B above Street
added.note: Mega-cap adtech beat with raised Q2 guide and continued AI/Axon-driven momentum; +2.9% AH but high-conviction major print with multi-tier-1 coverage.

added.title: Whirlpool Q1 2026 — revenue -10% to $3.27B, ongoing EPS swings to -$0.56; WHR drops 19.5%
added.note: Single-name -19.5% mover with named catalyst (Q1 miss + tariff/cost actions + lowered ongoing EPS profile).

sweeps.summary: Added two May 6 earnings/movers: Fortinet's Q1 2026 beat-and-raise (FTNT +15% AH) and CDW's Q1 2026 beat that triggered a ~19% sell-off on margin pressure.

added.title: Fortinet Q1 2026 — revenue $1.85B (+20%), non-GAAP EPS $0.82 vs $0.61 est, raises FY26 guide; FTNT +15% AH
added.note: Q1 print beat on revenue and EPS, raised FY26 guide; +15% post-close move and clear sector-leader read-through on AI-driven cybersecurity demand.

added.title: CDW Q1 2026 — revenue $5.68B beats but stock crashes ~19% as gross margin compresses 60bps to 21.0%
added.note: Headline beat but shares plunged ~19% on gross-margin compression — material single-name move that informs the IT reseller channel.

sweeps.summary: Four fresh adds: Healthpeak (DOC) Q1 with Janus Living IPO drives a 13.6% pop and a doubled FY26 EPS guide; Upstart (UPST) -7.9% as Q1 EBITDA margin halves on $104M marketing spend; Trump tariff refunds ($166B IEEPA) start paying out via CBP's CAPE portal; Bitcoin breaks $82K — highest since January — on $2.44B April ETF inflows.

added.title: Healthpeak Q1 2026 — FFO/sh $0.45 beats, raises FY26 EPS guide to $0.46–$0.50 after Janus Living IPO; DOC +13.6%
added.note: DOC ripped 13.6% on a Q1 FFO beat plus a >2x lift to FY26 EPS guide following the Janus Living IPO completion — significant for a REIT.

added.title: Upstart Q1 2026 — revenue +44% to $308M but adj EBITDA margin halves to 13% on $104M marketing spend; UPST -7.9%
added.note: Stock down ~7.9% with named catalyst (margin halving on marketing spend) and Piper Sandler PT cut — clean mover with primary-source-confirmed prints.

added.title: First wave of $166B Trump tariff refunds begins paying out via CBP CAPE system; refunds scheduled to ramp from May 12
added.note: First refund payouts of $166B in IEEPA tariffs began hitting importer accounts May 6 per Bloomberg; macro/regulatory event with broad consumer-discretionary read-through.

added.title: Bitcoin tops $82K — highest since January — as April spot ETF inflows hit $2.44B and Iran de-escalation boosts risk
added.note: Distinct from the existing May 4 $532M ETF flows item — this is the price-level breakout above $80K (highest since Jan 31) driven by April aggregate flows and Iran de-escalation.

sweeps.summary: Two qualifying adds for the May 7 morning sweep: Shell's Q1 print (beat on refining, smaller buyback) and Albemarle's Q1 print (lithium recovery delivers a 148% EBITDA jump and sends ALB +7% AH). Skipped Lyft, Coinbase, McDonald's: results not yet released as of sweep timestamp. Iran/oil and BTC catalysts already covered by existing items.

added.title: Shell Q1 2026 — adj earnings $6.9B beat $6.36B est on refining strength; trims buyback to $3B from $3.5B, hikes dividend 5%
added.note: First quarterly print from a supermajor for Q1 2026 in the feed; primary-source globenewswire press release with metrics and capital-return reset.

added.title: Albemarle Q1 2026 — sales +33% to $1.43B, adj EPS $2.95 trounces $1.19 est on lithium recovery; ALB +7% AH
added.note: Cleanest single-name read on the lithium price recovery; massive beat of consensus with concrete pricing/volume drivers and verifiable AH stock move.

sweeps.summary: One add: AB InBev Q1 2026 earnings beat (May 5). Backfill of a gap in prior sweeps — Corona-driven 20.8% EPS growth and 7.9% premarket move qualify as major. No other fresh qualifying items in the last ~100 minutes; McDonald's Q1 not yet released at sweep time.

added.title: AB InBev Q1 2026 — revenue +5.8% to $15.27B, underlying EPS +20.8% to $0.97 beats $0.91 est on Corona +16% growth; BUD +7.9% premarket
added.note: Backfill of May 5 print missed by prior sweep; 20.8% underlying EPS growth and 7.9% premarket move qualify as major. Within 72h cap (date 2026-05-05, sweep 2026-05-07T09:02Z).

sweeps.summary: Two fresh Q1 prints surfaced in the earnings stream: ADM (May 5) beat and raised FY26 guidance on biofuels-policy tailwinds, while Equinor (May 6) posted record production and an easy EPS beat that the market sold ~10% on heavier Norwegian tax timing.

added.title: ADM Q1 2026 — adj EPS $0.71 beats $0.66 est, raises FY26 guide to $4.15–$4.70 on biofuels tailwind; ADM +5.5% to 52-week high
added.note: Major-cap ag processor, EPS+revenue beat plus a ~15% midpoint guidance raise; stock to 52-wk high. Not in existing feed.

added.title: Equinor Q1 2026 — adj op income $9.77B, record output 2.31 mboe/d, adj EPS $1.48 trounces $1.01 est; EQNR -10.6% premarket on tax/cash drag
